linux-media.name: Linux Media
linux-media.description: Camera drivers, V4L2, ISP, MIPI CSI, sensors, libcamera, UVC
linux-media.thread_expert: Linux kernel media subsystem expert. Analyze this mailing list thread about camera drivers, V4L2, ISP, MIPI CSI, sensors, libcamera, or UVC.
linux-media.thread_tech_detail: Be specific about kernel structures, register layouts, pixel formats, DT properties, or API changes. Reference struct names, function names, format fourcc codes, DT compatible strings where relevant.
linux-media.synth_audience: developer who works on embedded Linux camera systems
linux-media.synth_focus: camera drivers, V4L2, ISP, MIPI CSI, sensors, libcamera, UVC, videobuf2
linux-media.synth_acronyms: CSI-2 (Camera Serial Interface), ISP (Image Signal Processor), DT (Device Tree), CCS (MIPI Camera Command Set)
linux-media.about_text: Focus: camera drivers, V4L2, ISP, MIPI CSI, sensors, libcamera, UVC, videobuf2

soc-bringup.name: SoC Bringup
soc-bringup.description: ARM SoC support, device tree, clock, pinctrl, power management, platform drivers
soc-bringup.thread_expert: Linux kernel ARM SoC platform expert. Analyze this mailing list thread about SoC bringup, device tree, clock/pinctrl/regulator subsystems, platform driver support, or hardware enablement.
soc-bringup.thread_tech_detail: Be specific about DT compatible strings, clock/reset controller IDs, register block offsets, SoC variant names, driver probe sequences, and binding properties. Reference struct names, DT node paths, and specific IP block versions where relevant.
soc-bringup.synth_audience: developer who works on ARM SoC bringup and embedded Linux platform support
soc-bringup.synth_focus: SoC bringup, device tree bindings, clock/pinctrl/power frameworks, platform drivers, new hardware enablement
soc-bringup.synth_acronyms: DT (Device Tree), SoC (System on Chip), PSCI (Power State Coordination Interface), GIC (Generic Interrupt Controller), SMMU (System Memory Management Unit), OPP (Operating Performance Points), ICC (InterConnect), PMIC (Power Management IC)
soc-bringup.about_text: Focus: ARM SoC bringup, device tree, clock/pinctrl/regulator, platform drivers, power management, new hardware support

jetson-tegra.name: Jetson / Tegra
jetson-tegra.description: NVIDIA Tegra/Jetson kernel support, GPU, camera, power management, device tree
jetson-tegra.thread_expert: NVIDIA Tegra/Jetson kernel developer. Analyze this mailing list thread about Tegra SoC support, Jetson platforms, GPU/camera/display drivers, host1x, BPMP, or device tree bindings.
jetson-tegra.thread_tech_detail: Be specific about Tegra SoC generations (T194/T234/T239), host1x channels, VI/CSI capture pipelines, BPMP ABI, clock/reset IDs, and DT compatible strings.
jetson-tegra.synth_audience: developer who works on NVIDIA Jetson embedded platforms and Tegra kernel support
jetson-tegra.synth_focus: Tegra SoC kernel drivers, Jetson camera pipelines, host1x/GPU, BPMP, device tree, power management
jetson-tegra.synth_acronyms: VI (Video Input), NVCSI (NVIDIA CSI), BPMP (Boot and Power Management Processor), EMC (External Memory Controller), SE (Security Engine)
jetson-tegra.about_text: Focus: NVIDIA Tegra/Jetson kernel support, camera VI/CSI pipelines, host1x GPU, BPMP, display, device tree

libcamera.name: libcamera / RPi Camera
libcamera.description: libcamera framework, Raspberry Pi camera stack, PiSP, ISP tuning, camera applications
libcamera.thread_expert: libcamera and Raspberry Pi camera stack expert. Analyze this mailing list thread about the libcamera framework, pipeline handlers, IPA modules, ISP tuning, RPi camera drivers, or camera applications.
libcamera.thread_tech_detail: Be specific about pipeline handler names, IPA algorithm implementations (AGC/AWB/CCM/LSC), sensor model numbers, PiSP front-end/back-end stages, control IDs, and pixel format handling.
libcamera.synth_audience: developer who works on embedded Linux camera systems, libcamera integration, or Raspberry Pi camera applications
libcamera.synth_focus: libcamera framework, pipeline handlers, IPA modules, Raspberry Pi camera stack (PiSP/Unicam/CFE), ISP tuning, sensor drivers
libcamera.synth_acronyms: IPA (Image Processing Algorithm), AGC (Auto Gain Control), AWB (Auto White Balance), CCM (Color Correction Matrix), LSC (Lens Shading Correction), PiSP (Pi Image Signal Processor), CFE (Camera Front End)
libcamera.about_text: Focus: libcamera framework, Raspberry Pi camera stack, IPA/ISP tuning, pipeline handlers, sensor drivers

dri-devel.name: DRM / GPU Graphics
dri-devel.description: DRM/KMS display, GPU drivers (amdgpu, i915, nouveau, panfrost), render nodes, GEM/TTM memory
dri-devel.thread_expert: Linux kernel DRM/KMS and GPU driver expert. Analyze this mailing list thread about GPU drivers (amdgpu, i915, nouveau, panfrost), DRM core, KMS display, GEM/TTM memory management, or GPU compute.
dri-devel.thread_tech_detail: Be specific about GPU IP block versions (GFX10, DCN, VCN), DRM object types (CRTC, plane, connector), GEM/TTM buffer operations, scheduler entities, firmware loading, and power management states.
dri-devel.synth_audience: developer who works on GPU drivers, display output, or GPU compute on embedded Linux
dri-devel.synth_focus: DRM/KMS drivers, amdgpu/RDNA, GPU memory management (GEM/TTM/DMA-BUF), display pipelines, GPU compute, scheduler
dri-devel.synth_acronyms: DRM (Direct Rendering Manager), KMS (Kernel Mode Setting), GEM (Graphics Execution Manager), TTM (Translation Table Manager), CRTC (Cathode Ray Tube Controller — scanout engine), DP (DisplayPort), DCN (Display Core Next)
dri-devel.about_text: Focus: DRM/KMS, GPU drivers (amdgpu, i915, nouveau, panfrost), display, GEM/TTM, GPU compute

linux-usb.name: Linux USB
linux-usb.description: USB subsystem, xHCI, UVC cameras, USB gadget, Type-C, USB PD
linux-usb.thread_expert: Linux kernel USB subsystem expert. Analyze this mailing list thread about USB host controllers (xHCI/DWC3), UVC cameras, USB gadget/configfs, Type-C/USB PD, or USB driver architecture.
linux-usb.thread_tech_detail: Be specific about USB descriptor layouts, endpoint types (isoc/bulk/interrupt), xHCI ring management, gadget configfs paths, Type-C port roles, and controller IP block versions.
linux-usb.synth_audience: developer who works on USB camera systems, USB gadget devices, or embedded USB controller drivers
linux-usb.synth_focus: USB host/device controllers, UVC cameras, USB gadget framework, Type-C/PD, xHCI/DWC3 drivers
linux-usb.synth_acronyms: UVC (USB Video Class), xHCI (eXtensible Host Controller Interface), DWC3 (DesignWare Core USB3), OTG (On-The-Go), TCPM (Type-C Port Manager), PD (Power Delivery), UCSI (USB Type-C Connector System Interface)
linux-usb.about_text: Focus: USB host controllers, UVC cameras, USB gadget, Type-C/PD, xHCI/DWC3

linux-riscv.name: Linux RISC-V
linux-riscv.description: RISC-V architecture support, SoC bringup, ISA extensions, boot flow, platform drivers
linux-riscv.thread_expert: Linux kernel RISC-V architecture expert. Analyze this mailing list thread about RISC-V platform support, SoC bringup (StarFive, T-Head, Sophgo), ISA extensions, SBI interface, or RISC-V specific driver work.
linux-riscv.thread_tech_detail: Be specific about ISA extension names (Zicbom, Zvbb, etc.), SoC model numbers, SBI call IDs, interrupt controller types (PLIC/APLIC/IMSIC), and device tree compatible strings for RISC-V platforms.
linux-riscv.synth_audience: developer tracking RISC-V Linux ecosystem for embedded and SoC platform work
linux-riscv.synth_focus: RISC-V SoC bringup, ISA extensions, platform support (StarFive/T-Head/Sophgo), SBI, boot flow, driver enablement
linux-riscv.synth_acronyms: SBI (Supervisor Binary Interface), PLIC (Platform-Level Interrupt Controller), AIA (Advanced Interrupt Architecture), IMSIC (Incoming MSI Controller), CSR (Control and Status Register), ISA (Instruction Set Architecture)
linux-riscv.about_text: Focus: RISC-V architecture, SoC bringup, ISA extensions, SBI, platform drivers, new hardware

devicetree.name: Device Tree
devicetree.description: Device tree bindings, DT schema, board DTS files, DT overlay, of_* API
devicetree.thread_expert: Linux kernel device tree binding expert. Analyze this mailing list thread about DT binding schemas, board DTS files, DT overlays, or the of_* kernel API for device tree parsing.
devicetree.thread_tech_detail: Be specific about binding property names, compatible strings, cell counts (#address-cells, #size-cells), phandle references, schema validation rules, and YAML binding structure. Reference specific SoC or IP block names.
devicetree.synth_audience: developer who writes device tree bindings and DTS files for embedded Linux SoC platforms
devicetree.synth_focus: DT binding schemas (YAML), board DTS/DTSI files, DT overlays, of_* API, compatible string conventions, new hardware bindings
devicetree.synth_acronyms: DT (Device Tree), DTS (Device Tree Source), DTSI (Device Tree Source Include), DTB (Device Tree Blob), FDT (Flattened Device Tree), DTBO (Device Tree Blob Overlay)
devicetree.about_text: Focus: Device tree bindings, DT schema, board DTS files, overlays, of_* API, new hardware bindings
